documentation.value: Basic YAML key-value pair. The key and value are separated by a colon followed by a space.
A mapping nested under a parent key using indentation (2 spaces).
A YAML sequence (ordered list) using block style with dash `-` indicators.
A YAML sequence written in flow (inline) style using square brackets.
A YAML mapping written in flow (inline) style using curly braces.
Literal block scalar preserves newlines. Each line break is kept as-is. Useful for pre-formatted text, scripts, or code blocks.
Folded block scalar folds newlines into spaces. Single newlines become spaces; blank lines become newlines. Useful for long text.
Literal block scalar with strip chomping indicator. Trailing newlines are removed from the final string.
Literal block scalar with keep chomping indicator. All trailing newlines are preserved in the final string.
Folded block scalar with strip chomping indicator. Trailing newlines are removed.
Folded block scalar with keep chomping indicator. All trailing newlines are preserved.
A YAML comment starts with `#`. Comments can appear on their own line or after a value.
YAML null can be expressed as `null`, `~`, or by leaving the value empty.
YAML boolean values: `true`, `false`, `yes`, `no`, `on`, `off` (YAML 1.1). In YAML 1.2 strict, only `true` and `false` are booleans.
Define a reusable node with an anchor `&name` and reference it elsewhere with an alias `*name`. Avoids duplication in YAML documents.
Merge key `<<` is used to merge mappings from an alias into the current mapping. Commonly used with anchors to share configuration.
The `---` marker indicates the start of a YAML document. Required when multiple documents exist in a single stream.
The `...` marker indicates the end of a YAML document without starting a new one.
A quoted string in YAML. Double quotes allow escape sequences (\n, \t, etc.). Single quotes treat content literally.
YAML integer types: decimal `123`, octal `0o14`, hexadecimal `0xC`.
YAML float types: fixed `1.23`, exponential `1.5e+3`, infinity `.inf`, not-a-number `.nan`.
YAML date in ISO 8601 format: `YYYY-MM-DD`.
YAML timestamp in ISO 8601 format: `YYYY-MM-DDThh:mm:ss±hh:mm`.
YAML binary data encoded as base64 string with the `!!binary` tag.
Define a Docker Compose service with image, ports, volumes, and environment variables.
Define named volumes for Docker Compose services.
Define custom networks for Docker Compose services to communicate.
Map host ports to container ports. Format: `HOST:CONTAINER` or `HOST:CONTAINER/protocol`.
Set environment variables for a Docker Compose service.
Express dependency between services. The dependent service starts after its dependencies.
Configure build context and Dockerfile for a service.

completions.label: Docker Compose: healthcheck

documentation.value: Configure a health check for a Docker Compose service to determine if it is running correctly.

completions.label: Docker Compose: deploy

documentation.value: Configure deployment options including replicas, resource limits, and restart policies (Swarm/Compose v3+).

completions.label: GitHub Actions: workflow

documentation.value: Complete GitHub Actions workflow with name, trigger, and a job definition.

completions.label: GitHub Actions: job

documentation.value: Define a job that runs on a specified runner with a sequence of steps.
A single step in a GitHub Actions job. Steps can run commands or use actions.
Reference a reusable action from the GitHub Marketplace or a repository.
Set environment variables at the workflow, job, or step level.
Trigger a workflow on a cron schedule. Uses POSIX cron syntax.

completions.label: GitHub Actions: workflow_dispatch

documentation.value: Allow manual triggering of a workflow from the GitHub UI with optional input parameters.

completions.insertText:
on:
  workflow_dispatch:
    inputs:
      ${1:input_name}:
        description: '${2:Description}'
        required: ${3|true,false|}
        default: '${4:default_value}'

documentation.value: Run a job across multiple configurations using a matrix strategy.
Reference a repository or organization secret in a workflow.
Conditionally run a step or job using an `if` expression.

completions.label: Kubernetes: Pod

documentation.value: A Kubernetes Pod is the smallest deployable unit, consisting of one or more containers.

completions.label: Kubernetes: Deployment

documentation.value: A Deployment manages a set of identical Pods, ensuring the desired number of replicas are running.

completions.label: Kubernetes: Service

documentation.value: A Service exposes a set of Pods as a network service with a stable IP and DNS name.

completions.label: Kubernetes: ConfigMap

documentation.value: A ConfigMap stores non-confidential configuration data as key-value pairs.

completions.label: Kubernetes: Secret

documentation.value: A Secret stores sensitive data such as passwords, tokens, or keys in base64-encoded form.

completions.label: Kubernetes: Ingress

documentation.value: An Ingress manages external HTTP/HTTPS access to services in a cluster, typically via routing rules.

completions.insertText:
apiVersion: networking.k8s.io/v1
kind: Ingress
metadata:
  name: ${1:my-ingress}
  annotations:
    nginx.ingress.kubernetes.io/rewrite-target: /
spec:
  rules:
    - host: ${2:example.com}
      http:
        paths:
          - path: ${3:/}
            pathType: Prefix
            backend:
              service:
                name: ${4:my-service}
                port:
                  number: ${5:80}

completions.label: Kubernetes: PersistentVolumeClaim

documentation.value: A PersistentVolumeClaim requests storage resources from the cluster.

completions.label: Kubernetes: Namespace

documentation.value: A Namespace provides a scope for resource names, enabling multi-tenancy within a cluster.

completions.insertText:
apiVersion: v1
kind: Namespace
metadata:
  name: ${1:my-namespace}

completions.label: Kubernetes: ServiceAccount

documentation.value: A ServiceAccount provides an identity for processes running in a Pod.

completions.label: Kubernetes: HorizontalPodAutoscaler

documentation.value: A HorizontalPodAutoscaler automatically scales the number of Pods based on observed CPU/memory utilization or custom metrics.

completions.insertText:
apiVersion: autoscaling/v2
kind: HorizontalPodAutoscaler
metadata:
  name: ${1:my-hpa}
spec:
  scaleTargetRef:
    apiVersion: apps/v1
    kind: Deployment
    name: ${2:my-deployment}
  minReplicas: ${3:1}
  maxReplicas: ${4:10}
  metrics:
    - type: Resource
      resource:
        name: cpu
        target:
          type: Utilization
          averageUtilization: ${5:80}

documentation.value: Define sequential stages for a CI/CD pipeline (e.g., build, test, deploy).
Define a job within a CI/CD pipeline stage with a script to execute.
Define artifacts to be saved after a job completes, available for download or later stages.
Cache dependencies or build outputs between pipeline runs to speed up execution.
Force a specific type interpretation using a YAML tag: `!!str`, `!!int`, `!!float`, `!!bool`, `!!null`, `!!binary`, etc.

completions.label: multi-document YAML
completions.detail: Multiple YAML documents in one file

documentation.value: YAML supports multiple documents in one file separated by `---`. Each document is independent.
A sequence where each item is a mapping (list of objects). Common pattern in YAML configurations.

completions.label: Docker Compose: full stack

documentation.value: Complete Docker Compose file with multiple services, volumes, and networks.

completions.label: GitHub Actions: on push/PR

documentation.value: Trigger a workflow on push and pull_request events for specific branches.

completions.insertText:
on:
  push:
    branches: [${1:main}]
    paths:
      - '${2:src/**}'
  pull_request:
    branches: [${1:main}]
